pi_items.verified_value: 16-bits/channel composite color (RGB) fluorescence microscopy image showing HeLa cells with red lysosomes, green mitochondria, and blue nucleus. Data range is 0-4095 (12-bit effective).

pi_items.source: https://imagej.net/ij/images/hela-cells.txt and PIL Image inspection showing mode='RGB' with 3 channels

pi_items.verified_value: Stored as 16-bit/channel (uint16) per RGB channel, but actual data values range 0-4095 (12-bit effective)
pi_items.source: Direct image analysis: TIFF BitsPerSample tag = (16,16,16), actual max value = 4095

pi_items.verified_value: 0.6 is a reasonable intermediate IoU threshold for cell segmentation tasks
pi_items.source: Cell segmentation literature: IoU thresholds range from 0.5 (lenient) to 0.95+, with 0.6 used as standard in various benchmarks

pi_items.verified_value: 15% is a reasonable tolerance reflecting typical variation in manual vs automated cell confluence measurement
pi_items.source: HeLa cell biology: typical confluence 50-90%, manual measurement variability literature shows ±10-15% is realistic

pi_items.verified_value: Approximately 49.51% using standard preprocessing pipeline (entropy filter + Otsu threshold + morphological cleanup)
pi_items.source: Replicated the ground truth generation script from setup_task.sh on the actual hela-cells.tif image

privileged_info_summary: The task uses the real ImageJ sample image hela-cells.tif (1.4MB, 512x672 pixels). The image is stored as 16-bit per channel RGB but contains only 12-bit data (max value 4095). Contrary to the task description claiming 'phase contrast', the official ImageJ documentation describes this as a composite fluorescence microscopy image with red lysosomes, green mitochondria, and blue nucleus. The calculated ground truth confluence is ~49.51% using entropy filtering and Otsu thresholding. The primary download URL (imagej.nih.gov) is broken (404), but the secondary URL (wsr.imagej.net) works. The IoU threshold of 0.6 and confluence tolerance of 15% are reasonable for cell segmentation tasks.
